Japanese: ‥‥はあ‥‥　はあ‥‥
‥‥くそっ！ なんでぼくがこんな目に‥‥
English: Argh! How did I get into this mess?

Japanese: ‥‥あのとき‥‥ぼくは どうして、あんなコトを‥‥！
English: Why...? Why did I do that...?

Japanese: ‥‥彼女とは、 もう会わないほうがいい。
English: That girl... You shouldn't see her anymore.

Japanese: そんなコト、 きみに言われたくないよ！
English: Hey! It's none of your business!

Japanese: ‥‥アンタのためなんだ。 きっと、よくないことが起こる。
English: I'm telling you for your sake. If you continue to see her, it's going to be bad news.

Japanese: う、ウソだッ！
English: Y-You're lying!

Japanese: いいか、聞くんだ。 ‥‥あの女はなぁ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥
English: Just listen to me. There's something you need to know about that girl... ...

Japanese: ‥‥やめてくれッ！
English: Stop it!

Japanese: 彼女のコト‥‥ そんなふうに言うなッ！
English: D-Don't talk about her like that!

Japanese: ぼ‥‥ぼくじゃないぞ！ こ、こんな‥‥こんな‥‥
English: It-It wasn't me! I-I d-didn't...

Japanese: ‥‥殺人なんて‥‥！
English: I didn't do it!

Japanese: ～５年前～ 綾里千尋・２回目の法廷
English: -- 5 Years Earlier -- Mia Fey 2nd Trial

Japanese: ４月１１日　午前　９時４０分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: April 11, 9:40 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: （い、いよいよね。 ‥‥キンチョーしちゃうな‥‥）
English: (Whew, it's finally time... I'm kind of nervous...)

Japanese: ウォッホォン！
English: *Ah-HHHHEM!*

Japanese: あ‥‥星影先生！ お、おはようございますっ！
English: Oh! Mr. Grossberg! Good morning!

Japanese: ‥‥千尋クン。 とにかく、まずは落ちつくんぢゃ。
English: Ah, Mia. Please calm yourself down!

Japanese: そんなキョロキョロしとったら、 挙動不審でタイホされちまうぞ。
English: You're going to get yourself arrested for suspicious behavior, you know.

Japanese: なな、何を言うんですか星影先生！ わた、私は落ちついてますっ！
English: What are you talking about!! I am relaxed, Mr. Grossberg!! Look at me, I'm relaxed!!

Japanese: うぐぐぐぐ。 む、胸ぐらをつかまんでくれ。
English: *Grrrmmphh* L-Let go of my lapels!

Japanese: ‥‥うう‥‥どうもチミはまだ、 そそっかしくてイカン。
English: Hmph... You obviously haven't got the temperament to be a lawyer.

Japanese: あ‥‥あの。今日はすみません！ 私なんかのために‥‥
English: I, err... I'm so sorry! It's just that I'm so nervous today...

Japanese: ‥‥なァに。 他ならぬチミの初舞台ぢゃ。
English: Oh, that's right. This is your first time in the big leagues, isn't it?

Japanese: この星影　宇宙ノ介（ほしかげ そらのすけ）‥‥力になるぞい！
English: Well, never you fear my dear. I, Marvin Grossberg, am at your service!

Japanese: あの。‥‥いちおう、法廷はこれで ２回目なんですけど‥‥。
English: Umm, actually this is my second time in court...

Japanese: しかし、チミには驚いたぞ。
English: Still, you surprised me... What, with your earnest request last night...

Japanese: 『この事件を担当させてほしい』 ‥‥ゆんべになって言い出すとは。
English: "Let me handle this case!" you suddenly said. And quite forcefully, too!

Japanese: きのう、初めて知ったんです。 この事件のこと‥‥。
English: I just found out yesterday. About the case, I mean.

Japanese: それで？　事件のデータは、 アタマに入っとるんぢゃろうな？
English: What? And you've already learned all the relevant facts?

Japanese: それは‥‥その。 もちろん大丈夫ですよ！　たぶん。
English: Well about that... You see... I mean, of course I have! I think.

Japanese: ヤレヤレ‥‥。
English: Oh dear...

Japanese: とにかく、そんなカオを 依頼人に見せないことぢゃ。
English: In any case, don't let our client see you're so nervous.

Japanese: ホレ。あの、情けなさそうな ボウヤぢゃろ？　今日の依頼人。
English: You see the poor young man in the pink sweater over there? That's our client.

Japanese: う。お。 おはよーございますセンセイッ！
English: *cough* *sniffle* Good morning there everybody!

Japanese: おはようございます。 （とりあえずエガオよ、千尋）
English: Good morning... (Try to keep smiling, Mia!)

Japanese: きょ、今日は‥‥ボク、 がんばりますからッ！
English: I, err, I just want to say... I'll give it all I've got!

Japanese: ええもお、やりますとも！ げほ。げほげほ。
English: Yup, it'll be fine! No prob! *cough* *achoo* *achoo*

Japanese: あ、‥‥そんなにリキまないで。 ええと‥‥なるほどさん！
English: Oh, what's wrong? Do you have a cold or something... Mr. Wry?

Japanese: はは、はいッ！ ‥‥あ。いえ、あの。
English: Actually, it's Wright... Like the flying brothers... People screw it up all the time.

Japanese: セイカクには“なるほどう” なんですけど‥‥
English: And yes I have a cold. That's what this mask is for.
My doc says this way, I won't give it to anyone else... Be kind to others, he says...

Japanese: いいですか、なるほどさん！ シンパイはいりません。
English: Right, Mr. Wright! You have nothing to fear in court today!

Japanese: あなたが無実なら‥‥ かならず、助けてみせます！
English: If you are truly innocent... I promise I will save you!

Japanese: うぐぐぐぐ。む、胸ぐらを つかまないでください‥‥げほ。
English: Nnnnggghh... P-Please l-let go of my shirt... *cough*

Japanese: （‥‥そうよ。ホントに おびえてるのは、彼のほう）
English: (That's right, he's the one on trial, not you! He's the one who should be nervous!)

Japanese: （私がシッカリしなくちゃね ‥‥千尋！）
English: (You need to stay strong for your client, Mia!)

Japanese: 私は綾里　千尋（あやさとちひろ） ‥‥新米弁護士。
English: My name is Mia Fey. I'm still pretty new at this lawyer thing...

Japanese: 初めて法廷に立ったのは、 今から１年前のことだった。
English: The first time I appeared in court was a year ago.

Japanese: 二度と立ちなおれないほどの ダメージを受けた、あの裁判‥‥
English: But that trial traumatized me so badly, I thought I'd never set foot in another courtroom.

Japanese: あれから１年。私は今、 ふたたび法廷に立とうとしている。
English: It's been one year since then, and well, here I am again.

Japanese: 今日こそ‥‥ 今度こそ、私は負けない。
English: But this time... This time I'll win.

Japanese: ‥‥依頼人のために。 そして‥‥私自身のために。
English: For my client... And for myself.

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: April 11, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: では、成歩堂　龍一（なるほどう りゅういち）の法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しております！
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor!

Japanese: 検察側、準備完了しております。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 綾里　千尋さん‥‥でしたな？
English: The defense today is... Ms... Ms... Mia Fey, was it?

Japanese: は、はい。なんでしょうか。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor. Is there a problem?

Japanese: 本件の担当弁護士は、たしか 星影氏だったのでは？
English: I was under the impression that Marvin Grossberg was to be leading the defense.

Japanese: え！　あ。それがその。
English: Yes! Well, you see...

Japanese: 星影弁護士はちょっと、 急用ができてしまって‥‥
English: Mr. Grossberg had... A-A bit of an emergency...

Japanese: 急用？　あなたのお隣で ボンヤリしているではないですか！
English: Emergency? But isn't that him standing there right next to you?

Japanese: はい、まあ‥‥。
English: Yes, well...

Japanese: あなた‥‥たしか、新人でしたな。 本当に大丈夫なのですか？
English: You... You're just a rookie. Are you sure you can really handle this?

Japanese: （‥‥こ、ここで負けちゃダメ。 ニラみつけてやるの、千尋！）
English: (Don't let him scare you, Mia! Give him your toughest look!)

Japanese: モチロン大丈夫ですわ！ ‥‥たぶん。
English: Of course, Your Honor! I think.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥では、亜内検事。 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Well, Mr. Payne. Your opening statement, please.

Japanese: ふう、やれやれ‥‥。
English: Well, well, well...

Japanese: 私ほどのベテラン検事が、 おじょうさんの子守とはねえ。
English: I can't believe a veteran like me has to spend his time baby- sitting a new defense lawyer.

Japanese: まあ、今日は勝ち負けは気にせず、 ムネを借りるつもりで来なさい！
English: Don't worry little girl. It will all be over soon.

Japanese: （ナニよそれ‥‥）
English: (What was that all about? Was he trying to trash-talk me?)

Japanese: では、事件のあらましを 説明させていただきましょう。
English: Now then, I'd like to proceed with a summary of events on the day in question.

Japanese: 事件があったのは、私立・勇盟 （ゆうめい）大学のキャンパス。
English: The incident occurred on the campus of Ivy University.

Japanese: 殺害されたのは、呑田　菊三 （のんだきくぞう）くん。
English: The murder victim was a student named Doug Swallow.

Japanese: 薬学部の４回生だったそうです。
English: He was a fourth year student studying Pharmacology.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥いわゆる、 インテリゲンチャですな。
English: Hmm... It sounds like he was a very bright young man.

Japanese: さて。‥‥これが、現場にて 撮影された写真です。
English: Yes, well, next we have a photo taken at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: 事件は、その発生直後、学生に よって発見、通報されました。
English: Students discovered the scene shortly after the murder. They found the victim's body...

Japanese: 現場には、被害者の死体と‥‥ 逃げおくれた被告人がいたのです。
English: ...and the defendant, who had obviously bungled his getaway. They then called the police.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。それは、 すこぶるアヤシイですな。
English: Hmm... That certainly makes the defendant look very suspicious indeed.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 その写真を証拠として受理します。
English: Very well. The court accepts this photo into the record as evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《現場写真・１》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Crime Photo 1 added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥ところで。
English: By the way...

Japanese: この写真を見るかぎり、 被害者の死因がわかりませんな。
English: I can't quite tell the cause of death from this photo.

Japanese: ふっふっふっ‥‥
English: Hee, hee, hee...

Japanese: さすが、裁判長どの。 なかなかスルドイ‥‥
English: Your reputation for sagacity is well-earned, Your Honor.

Japanese: ジッサイこの被害者は、ちょっと 変わった死にかたをしとるのです。
English: The truth is that this victim died a rather unusual death.

Japanese: “変わった死にかた”‥‥！
English: An unusual death...!?

Japanese: いったい、どんな！
English: What do you mean, Mr. Payne!?

Japanese: それは‥‥弁護人に お答えねがいましょうかな。
English: Well... Perhaps the defense would like to take this question.

Japanese: カンタンな質問で、キンチョーを ほぐしてさしあげる‥‥
English: A simple question. I thought I might loosen you up a bit.

Japanese: これぞ、ゼントルメンのヤサシサ ‥‥というヤツですな。
English: I am a genteel-man, if you will.

Japanese: ぜ、ぜんとるめん‥‥
English: Um, a what?

Japanese: （ナメられちゃダメよ千尋！ ここは一発、カマしておくの！）
English: (Stand up to him, Mia! Show him what you're made of!)

Japanese: そうぢゃ！　パシッと 答えてやるんぢゃ！　死因を！
English: Ah, a perfect opportunity! Well, what was it? The cause! Go on!

Japanese: ‥‥って、なんぢゃ！ わからんのかいッ！
English: ...Please say you know at least this much.

Japanese: ご、ごめんなさいっ！ 私‥‥資料まで手が回らなくて。
English: I-I'm so sorry... I-I didn't get a chance to read through the whole file.

Japanese: むぐぐ‥‥シリが痛みだしたワイ。
English: *groan* My hemorrhoids are beginning to act up...

Japanese: ‥‥いいかの。
English: Now see here!

Japanese: 事件に関する資料は、すべて 《法廷記録》にファイルされとる。
English: The details of the case are filed under the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥それぐらいは 知っとるぢゃろうな？
English: But you knew that already, didn't you?

Japanese: （法廷記録！　たしか、Ｒボタン で見ることができるはずね‥‥）
English: (Ah! The Court Record! I think I can see that by touching the Court Record Button...)

Japanese: ワシらが法廷で闘うための武器は、 《法廷記録》の中に必ずある。
English: All of the weapons we need can be found in the Court Record.

Japanese: まずはそのデータをよく見て、 よーく考えるんぢゃ！
English: Take a good, hard look at the data there and think carefully before you answer, m'dear!

Japanese: は、はいっ！ よーく考えてみますっ！
English: Y-Yes, sir! I'll do just that!

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥あわてちゃダメ！ 検事さんの思うツボだもんね）
English: (I've got to stay calm! I can't let that prosecutor get the better of me!)

Japanese: （法廷記録‥‥見てみよう！ Ｒボタンで‥‥）
English: (The Court Record... OK, let's take a look! I just touch the Court Record Button here...)

Japanese: では、弁護人に 答えていただきましょう。
English: Now then, would the attorney for the defense please answer the question?

Japanese: この事件の被害者の死因は なんですかな？
English: What was the cause of death?

Japanese: それはもう‥‥ちっそく死、 ではないでしょうか。
English: Well... I think you'll find it was a case of asphyxiation.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ “ちっそく”とねえ。
English: Hmm... Asphyxiation, huh?

Japanese: ちちち千尋クン！ ナニを言っとるんぢゃい！
English: M-Mia! What are you saying!?

Japanese: この法廷の重い空気‥‥ 私、もうイキが詰まりそうで。
English: Well it's stuffy in here! I can hardly breathe.

Japanese: ダレもチミの気分など聞いとらん！ ちゃんと法廷記録を見るんぢゃ！
English: No one's asking how you feel! Please, make sure to properly read the Court Record!

Japanese: ご、ごめんなさいっ！ ちゃんと見てみますっ！
English: I-I'm so sorry! I'll take a better look!

Japanese: （しっかり、千尋！ 法廷記録は‥‥Ｒボタンよね）
English: (Hang in there, Mia! Open the Court Record with the Court Record Button...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 ではもう一度、聞きますぞ。
English: Hmm... I'll ask you once again.

Japanese: ええと‥‥凍死、でしょうか。
English: Err... Hypothermia, I believe.

Japanese: あの、星影先生。
English: Umm, Mr. Grossberg?

Japanese: ‥‥私、急に サムくなってきたんですけど‥‥
English: Does it suddenly feel very cold in here to you?

Japanese: チミの答えが 法廷を凍りつかせちゃったんぢゃ！
English: That's because your absurd reply has brought a chill to the courtroom!

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥
English: I-I'm so sorry...

Japanese: 法廷記録をキチンと見て 答えるんぢゃ！
English: Look at the Court Record more carefully, would you!

Japanese: 判決なんて、それはもう はかないモンぢゃからの！
English: How can you expect to win a case this way!?

Japanese: （しっかり、千尋！ 法廷記録は‥‥Ｒボタンよね）
English: (Hang in there, Mia! Open the Court Record with the Court Record Button...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 ではもう一度、聞きますぞ。
English: Hmm... I'll ask you once again.

Japanese: 法廷記録によると、感電‥‥ そのショックで亡くなっています。
English: According to the Court Record, it was a fatal electric shock. In other words, electrocution.

Japanese: か、感電‥‥ですか。 しかし、いったいどうやって！
English: Electrocution? Hmm... But how could such a thing happen?

Japanese: 大型のスタンガンでも 使ったのですかな？
English: Did the murderer use some type of new, super-powerful stun-gun, perhaps?

Japanese: それは‥‥審理が進むにつれて アキラカになるでしょう。
English: The answer to that will become crystal clear as this trial proceeds, Your Honor.

Japanese: その前に。重要なポイントが もう１つ、あるのですよ。
English: But before that, there is one more vital issue.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥？
English: Wh-What's that?

Japanese: “動機”ですよ、モチロン。
English: Why, motive of course.

Japanese: 被告と被害者のあいだには、 あるトラブルがあったのです。
English: Apparently there was some bad blood between the victim and the defendant.

Japanese: とらぶる‥‥？
English: Bad blood...?

Japanese: そ、それはいったい‥‥
English: Wh-What do you mean...?

Japanese: おっと‥‥！
English: Oopsie! I'm terribly sorry.

Japanese: あなたも弁護士なら、それぐらい トーゼン、押さえているはず。
English: You're the defense attorney, so you must know all about it.

Japanese: ‥‥そうでしょうな？
English: I shouldn't be stealing your spotlight like this.

Japanese: （イヤにカラんでくるわね ‥‥この検事さん）
English: (I really don't like this guy's smug attitude...)

Japanese: それが、あの男‥‥ 亜内検事のやりかたなんぢゃ。
English: That's Winston Payne for you. He is one smooth operator, if you catch my drift.

Japanese: “新人つぶし”の異名を とっておる。
English: They don't call him the "Rookie Killer" for nothing, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、弁護人に お聞きしましょう。
English: Now then, let's hear from the defense.

Japanese: 被告人と被害者のあいだに、 どんなトラブルがあったか？
English: What was the source of the bad blood between the victim and the defendant?

Japanese: それを示す《証拠品》を 提示していただきます！
English: And this time, I would like to see some supporting evidence!

Japanese: しょ、“証拠品”‥‥？
English: Ev-Evidence...?

Japanese: あわてることはない。
English: Ah, no need to get all worked up over this.

Japanese: ワシらの武器はつねに、 法廷記録の中にある。
English: As I said, all our weapons can be found in the Court Record.

Japanese: 正しいデータを、ヒゲのジイさんに たたきつけてやるんぢゃ！
English: Find the evidence you need and then shove it into Ol' Graybeard's face!

Japanese: は、はいっ！ ヒゲのおジイさんですね！
English: Y-Yes, sir! Into Ol' Graybeard's face!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと星影さん。
English: Err, Mr. Grossberg.

Japanese: 新人教育には、 もっと気をつかうように！
English: Try to set a better example for the young lady!

Japanese: 法廷記録には、《証拠品》の他に 《関係者》のデータもある。
English: Mia, evidence isn't the only thing in the Court Record. People's profiles are as well.

Japanese: Ｒボタンで切り替えられるから、 ゼンブ目を通して考えるのぢゃぞ！
English: You can toggle between Profiles and Evidence, so be sure to go over it all!

Japanese: ‥‥それでは 示していただきましょう。
English: Now then, let's see what you've got.

Japanese: 成歩堂くんと被害者のトラブル。 ‥‥その原因と考えられるのは？
English: What was the cause of the bad blood between Phoenix Wright and the victim?

Japanese: ‥‥星影さん。
English: Mr. Grossberg...

Japanese: この弁護‥‥、やはりあなたが 担当するべきでした。
English: I honestly think it would be best for the defense if you were in charge.

Japanese: この弁護人は、審理のトラブルの 原因になりかねません。
English: I'm afraid Ms. Fey might stir up some bad blood with this court if she were to continue.

Japanese: “ハズレ”ということぢゃな。 ‥‥カンタンに言えば。
English: Or simply put, you are quite mistaken, m'dear.

Japanese: うううう‥‥すみません‥‥
English: Oh no... I'm so sorry...

Japanese: 新人だからといって イイカゲンな発言は困ります。
English: Even if you are new, your lack of preparation is inexcusable.

Japanese: もう一度、考えなおしてください。
English: Now take a moment and think it over again.

Japanese: は、はいっ！ （大丈夫。まだやれるわ、千尋！）
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor! (It's OK! You've got another chance, Mia!)

Japanese: トラブルの原因として 考えられるのは、この人物です。
English: The reason for the bad blood between the two of them was... this woman here.

Japanese: 美柳（みやなぎ）　ちなみさん ‥‥ですか。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne, is it?

Japanese: ‥‥さすがに、それぐらいは 心得ているようですな‥‥弁護人。
English: Very good, Ms. Fey. You seem to have picked up on at least this much.

Japanese: 彼女は現在、 被告人・成歩堂くんの恋人です。
English: This woman is the girlfriend of the defendant, Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: しかし。半年前までは、被害者の 呑田くんとつきあっていた。
English: But up until about eight months ago, she was with the victim, Mr. Swallow.

Japanese: 今回の事件に、彼女の存在が カラんでいるのは明白でしょう‥‥
English: Clearly she has some part to play in this story.

Japanese: ふむむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥さすが、亜内検事ぢゃな。
English: Ah, he's done it again.

Japanese: 証人の尋問に入る前に、裁判長の 印象を決定づけようとしておる。
English: Before the cross-examination starts, he's already got the judge thinking like he wants.

Japanese: それでは、亜内検事！ 最初の証人を呼んでください。
English: Very well, Mr. Payne. Please call your first witness!

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂　龍一くんを おねがいいたしましょうか。
English: If it pleases the court, the prosecution would like to call Mr. Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: ひ‥‥被告人自身、ですか‥‥ いかがですかな？　弁護人。
English: What? The defendant himself? Well, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: （大丈夫！　なるほどさんは 無実なんだから‥‥）
English: (It's fine! After all, Mr. Wright is innocent, right?)

Japanese: ‥‥異議はありません。
English: The defense has no objection.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: それでは‥‥ 成歩堂　龍一を証人席へ！
English: The court calls Mr. Phoenix Wright to the witness stand!

Japanese: では、証人。職業と名前を。
English: Witness, please state your name and occupation.

Japanese: は、はい。 名前は‥‥成歩堂　龍一です。
English: Oh, ah, yes... My name is Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: 職業‥‥今は、その。 容疑者、やってますけど。
English: My job is, um... Well, right now, I guess I'm a suspect.

Japanese: いやいや。タイホされる前の 職業をおねがいします。
English: No, no. He means what did you do before you were arrested?

Japanese: あ。‥‥げほげほ。 だ、大学生です。
English: Oh... *achoo* *achoo* *achoo* I was a university student.

Japanese: さて、成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: きみの容疑は、同じ大学の学友・ 呑田　菊三くんの殺害‥‥
English: You understand that you are suspected in the death of your fellow student, Doug Swa--

Japanese: あ‥‥ボク！ アヤしいモノじゃないんです！
English: But! But I didn't do it! I'm innocent I tell you!

Japanese: だって、ボクはげほ。 げほげほげほ。げほげほげほげほげ
English: I'm telling you I was... *achoo* *achoo* *cough* *cough* *achoo* *cough*

Japanese: 被告人。 ‥‥カゼをうつさないように。
English: Would the defendant please refrain from passing on his cold to the rest of us!

Japanese: どうやら証人は、よっぽど 証言したいコトがあるようですな。
English: It seems the witness has something he wants to say.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥では、被告人。
English: Hmm... Well then, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 被害者・呑田　菊三くんとの カンケイを証言してください。
English: Please tell us about your relation to the victim.

Japanese: はいッ！
English: Right away, Your Honor!

Japanese: ～被害者との関係～
English: -- The Victim and I --

Japanese: あの、ボク‥‥。たしかに あそこにいましたけど‥‥
English: Um, I... I admit I was there...

Japanese: 殺したなんて、とんでもない！ 死体を見つけただけなんです！
English: But I'm not a killer! All I did was find his body!

Japanese: そもそもアイツのコト、 よく知らなかったし‥‥。
English: I hardly knew the guy to begin with...

Japanese: あんなキザなイギリスかぶれ、 ボク、話したこともないですよ！
English: I never even talked to that stuck-up British wannabe!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。被害者のことは、 よく知らなかった‥‥なるほど。
English: Hmm... I see. So you hardly knew the victim?

Japanese: ええ！　だってボク、 アヤしいモノじゃないですからね！
English: Right! Like I said, I'm not a killer!

Japanese: （よかった‥‥。裁判長さん、 わかってくれたみたいね）
English: Whew... It looks like the judge understands.

Japanese: むうう‥‥。 やはりアマいのお、千尋クン。
English: Mmfph... You're being naïve, you know. Too naïve.
Huh...?

Japanese: うふふふふふふ‥‥おじょうさん。
English: Hee, hee, hee, hee, hee.

Japanese: １つ、忘れていることが あるようですな。
English: It seems that you've forgotten one small thing, young lady.

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: And that would be...?

Japanese: この証人に、《尋問》を してもらわないとねえ‥‥。
English: This witness still has to undergo something called cross-examination.

Japanese: じんもん‥‥？
English: Cross-examination...?

Japanese: そのとおり。弁護士には、 尋問のギムがあるのぢゃ。
English: He's right. And it's the defense's duty to carry out the cross-examination.

Japanese: 証人の発言にムジュンがないか どうか、たしかめるために‥‥な。
English: The purpose is to determine if a witness's testimony contains any contradictions.

Japanese: “ムジュン”ですって‥‥
English: Contradictions...?

Japanese: 証人がウソをついていれば、当然 法廷記録と食いちがいが生まれる。
English: If a witness is lying, their statements will conflict with the Court Record.

Japanese: でも‥‥なるほどさんは 私の依頼人なんですよ！
English: But... Mr. Wright is my client!

Japanese: たとえそれが依頼人であっても‥‥ ムジュンは暴かなくてはならん。
English: Even if he is your client, in court, all lies must be struck down.

Japanese: それが弁護士のギム、なのぢゃ。
English: As a lawyer, that is your duty, you see.

Japanese: （どういうコト？ もしかして、今の証言には‥‥）
English: (What does he mean by that? Is he saying that testimony just now...)

Japanese: （ウソが‥‥ “ムジュン”があると言うの？）
English: (That there was a lie -- a contradiction?)

Japanese: それでは‥‥ 弁護人に、尋問を命じます！
English: Now then, your cross- examination, if you please, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: （そんなハズ、ないわよね‥‥ なるほどさん！）
English: (Please, Mr. Wright... Tell me you haven't been lying!)

Japanese: （あなたが、 この私にウソをつくなんて‥‥）
English: (You wouldn't do that to me... would you...?)

Japanese: ～被害者との関係～
English: -- The Victim and I --

Japanese: あの、ボク‥‥。たしかに あそこにいましたけど‥‥
English: Um, I... I admit I was there...

Japanese: 殺したなんて、とんでもない！ 死体を見つけただけなんです！
English: But I'm not a killer! All I did was find his body!

Japanese: そもそもアイツのコト、 よく知らなかったし‥‥。
English: I hardly knew the guy to begin with...

Japanese: あんなキザなイギリスかぶれ、 ボク、話したこともないですよ！
English: I never even talked to that stuck-up British wannabe!

Japanese: どうぢゃ、千尋クン。 ひとりでイケそうかね？
English: Well m'dear, do you think you can manage on your own from this point?

Japanese: あの‥‥星影先生。 ちょっと、私‥‥
English: Umm, Mr. Grossberg... I could use a little help...

Japanese: うむむ。そんな目で見ちゃイカン。 ‥‥セツなくなっちまう。
English: Ah, please don't look so sad. It brings a tear to my eye when you do.

Japanese: いいかの。キホンは “証言”と“法廷記録”‥‥
English: Alright, listen carefully. By comparing the testimony to the Court Record...

Japanese: この２つをよおく見くらべて、 ムジュンを探すんぢゃ。
English: ...you should be able to discover any contradictions, if there are any.

Japanese: そして、ムジュンを見つけたら‥‥
English: And when you've found one...

Japanese: その証言と食いちがう法廷記録の データを《つきつける》‥‥
English: I present the piece of evidence that contradicts the witness's testimony...?

Japanese: そう。そのとおりぢゃ。
English: Yes. That's the ticket!

Japanese: でも‥‥。 この証言に、ムジュンなんて！
English: But still... I don't see any contradictions in Mr. Wright's testimony!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥情報が 足りないのかもしれんな。
English: Hmm... Maybe you don't have enough information yet.

Japanese: 情報が‥‥？
English: Information...?

Japanese: 《ゆさぶる》のも手ぢゃな。 オドしつけてやるんぢゃ。
English: You can press for more information by leaning on a witness.

Japanese: 自分の依頼人を、ですか‥‥？
English: Even if they're my own client?

Japanese: ダレであれ、アヤしいヤツは ガクガクゆさぶる。
English: It doesn't matter who it is, if you think they're lying, press them like a cheap suit!

Japanese: ‥‥これは弁護士の宿命ぢゃ。
English: That is the obligation of an attorney.

Japanese: （もう一度よ、千尋！ 最初から証言を聞いてみるの！）
English: (OK, Mia. One more time, from the very beginning of his testimony!)

Japanese: （１年前に一度‥‥ 私は法廷に立っているわ）
English: (One year ago... I was in a courtroom just like this.)

Japanese: （これぐらい‥‥ヒトリで やってみせる！）
English: (I can do it. I can handle this myself!)

Japanese: むうう‥‥くれぐれも ムリしちゃイカンぞ、千尋クン。
English: Mmrgh... You mustn't try to bite off more than you can chew, Mia.

Japanese: だ、大丈夫です！　やるべきことは わかっていますから。
English: I-I'll be fine! I know what I have to do!

Japanese: 情報を引き出すために《ゆさぶる》 ‥‥そして、もう１つ。
English: Remember, you can always press him to get more information. Oh, and one more thing.

Japanese: 決定的なムジュンを暴きだすために 《つきつける》‥‥
English: When you're going to state a contradiction, make sure you present some definitive proof.

Japanese: （もう一度よ、千尋！ 最初から証言を聞いてみるの！）
English: (OK, Mia. One more time, from the very beginning of his testimony!)

Japanese: シャツの背中に描かれてたんです。 バカでかいユニオン・ジャックが。
English: He was always walking around with a huge Union Jack on the back of his shirt.

Japanese: “あそこ”‥‥というのは、 被害者が殺害された現場ですね？
English: When you say "there", you mean the place where the victim was murdered?

Japanese: え、ええ、まあ。 ナニがアレされた場所です、ハイ。
English: Y-Yeah, sort of. The place where SOMETHING happened anyway.

Japanese: ボカしてもムダですぞ。 ‥‥写真があるのですからな。
English: "Something"? You can't hide what happened. We have photographic evidence.

Japanese: げほ。げほがほげほげはげほごほ。
English: *achoo* *achoo* *achoo* *achoo*

Japanese: そ、それで‥‥なるほどさん。
English: A-Anyway, Mr. Wright...

Japanese: どうしてあなたは、あの現場に いたのかしら？
English: What were you doing at the scene of the crime?

Japanese: 被害者の呑田さんのコトは‥‥ 知らなかったんでしょう？
English: I thought you said you didn't know the victim, Mr. Swallow.

Japanese: それはホラ。グーゼンですよ！ グーゼン、通りかかったんです。
English: It was just a coincidence! We bumped into each other by accident.

Japanese: ‥‥グーゼン、ねえ‥‥
English: A coincidence, huh...?

Japanese: 死体を見つけた‥‥ということは、 警察への通報は、あなたが？
English: You say you found the body? So who called the police?

Japanese: え！　‥‥げほ。げほげほげほ。
English: Huh!? Um... *achoo* *cough* *achoo* *cough* *achoo* *cough*

Japanese: ザンネンながら‥‥ 通報したのは、他の学生たちです。
English: Unfortunately it was some other students that notified the police.

Japanese: “学生たち”‥‥？
English: "Other students"...?

Japanese: そのとおり。 彼らは、目撃しているのです。
English: That's correct. They were witnesses.

Japanese: 被害者のそばにボーゼンと 立ちすくむ、被告人を！
English: Witnesses who saw the defendant standing there, next to the body, in shock!

Japanese: そ、そうなのですか！　被告人！
English: W-What! Is this true, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: げ‥‥げほっ！　げほげほげほ。
English: *achoo* *achoo* *achoo* *achoo* *achoo* *achoo*

Japanese: （コトバに詰まったらセキこむの、 やめてくれないかしら‥‥）
English: (Could you stop sneezing every time you're in a bind...?)

Japanese: た、たしかに、死体を見つけて ビックリしちゃったんですけど。
English: W-Well, it's true that I was pretty shocked when I found the body.

Japanese: でも、ボク‥‥
English: But, but I...

Japanese: カオも名前も‥‥ ゼンゼン知らなかったわけですね？
English: So, you didn't know his face, or even his name, right?

Japanese: はいっ！　あ。いえ‥‥その。 そういうワケでは‥‥
English: Right! Um, well, no... That is... I mean...

Japanese: え。‥‥どういうことですか？
English: So... Which is it? Did you know him or not?

Japanese: げほ。げほげほ。
English: *achoo* *achoo* *achoo*

Japanese: コラ！　セキでごまかさない！
English: Now, see here! You can't avoid answering the question by sneezing all day!

Japanese: ううう‥‥その。ええと‥‥ 名前ぐらいは知ってました。
English: Err... Umm, well... I guess I did know his name.

Japanese: （は‥‥初耳だわ‥‥）
English: (N-News to me! Why didn't he tell me that before...?)

Japanese: あの‥‥ちいちゃんと、ムカシ つきあっていたオトコだ、って。
English: Um... I heard he used to date Dollie.

Japanese: “ちいちゃん”‥‥というのは？
English: Who is this "Dollie" person?

Japanese: まあ、被告人のコイビト‥‥ 美柳　ちなみさんのことでしょう。
English: Ah yes, that would be the defendant's lover, Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: おほ。それはちょっと、こう‥‥ あまずっぱい感じがしますねえ。
English: Oh, I see. Ah, young love... So bittersweet...

Japanese: でも、それだけですッ！
English: But that's all I knew about him!

Japanese: なるほどさん。 あなた、こう証言しましたね。
English: Mr. Wright, you stated the following in your testimony:

Japanese: “被害者のことはよく知らない” ‥‥って。
English: "I hardly knew the guy to begin with..."

Japanese: そ、そうです！　だってボク、 アイツなんか‥‥
English: Th-That's right! I mean why would I even--

Japanese: でも‥‥それはちょっと、 ヘンじゃないかしら。
English: But that doesn't sound right.

Japanese: それならなぜ‥‥ あなたは知っているのですか？
English: If you hardly knew him...

Japanese: 被害者が “イギリスかぶれ”だったコトを！
English: ...then why would you say that the victim was a "stuck-up British wannabe"!?
*achoo*

Japanese: なんでそこでダマるの！
English: Well, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: あ！　いや！　ボク、決して アヤしいモノじゃないですからッ！
English: Ah! No! It wasn't me! I'm not a killer, I swear!

Japanese: 被告人。それならば、 証言に加えていただきましょう。
English: Mr. Wright. I will give you an opportunity to revise your testimony.

Japanese: なぜ、被害者が“イギリスかぶれ” だと知っていたか‥‥？
English: How is it that you knew the victim was, as you put it, a "British wannabe"...?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: Y-Yes, well...

Japanese: 現場で、それを見たわけですか？ ‥‥ユニオン・ジャックを。
English: Did you see it at the crime scene? The Union Jack, I mean.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですよ！
English: Y-Yes, that's right! I saw it at the crime scene!

Japanese: だから‥‥きっとイギリスが 大好きなんだろうなあ、って。
English: That's why... That's why I figured he must love British stuff, see?

Japanese: あ！　ウソじゃありませんから！ ボク、アヤしいモノじゃないし‥‥
English: It's true! Cross my heart! I swear I didn't do it...

Japanese: （‥‥アヤしすぎる‥‥）
English: (He's acting fishier than the salmon I ate last night...)

Japanese: ちょっといいですか、弁護人！
English: May I ask you something, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: は、はいっ！ （なな、ナニかしら突然‥‥）
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor. (What is it now?)

Japanese: ‥‥その。ダレのコトですかな？ “ゆにおん・ぢゃっく”とは‥‥
English: Who is this person anyway? This "Union Jack" fellow...

Japanese: ユニオン・ジャックというのは、 イギリスの国旗のコトです。
English: The Union Jack is the name of the flag of England.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。ニッポンにおける “ひのまる”みたいな？
English: Oh, I see... So you mean like the "Stars and Stripes", right?

Japanese: さすがは、裁判長どの。 ご理解がすばやい‥‥
English: As usual, Your Honor, your insight astounds me.

Japanese: （‥‥そんなコトよりも‥‥）
English: (Hey, something just occurred to me...)

Japanese: （今の証言‥‥ どこか、おかしくないかしら？）
English: (Isn't there something strange about this bit just now?)

Japanese: それが“ムジュン”ぢゃよ。 ‥‥千尋クン。
English: Mia... There is a contradiction here!

Japanese: せ、先生‥‥！
English: M-Mr. Grossberg!

Japanese: つきつけてやるんぢゃ。 あのバカなボウヤに‥‥証拠品を！
English: Quickly now! Show that boy you mean business! With evidence, I mean!

Japanese: （法廷記録ね‥‥ チェックしてみるの、千尋！）
English: (OK, Mia, check the Court Record carefully!)

Japanese: なるほどさん。 あなた、こう証言しましたね。
English: Mr. Wright, you stated the following in your testimony:

Japanese: “被害者のことはよく知らない” ‥‥って。
English: "I hardly knew the guy to begin with..."

Japanese: そ、そうです！　だってボク、 アイツなんか‥‥
English: Th-That's right! I mean why would I even--

Japanese: でも‥‥それはちょっと、 ヘンじゃないかしら。
English: But that doesn't sound right.

Japanese: それならなぜ‥‥ あなたは知っているのですか？
English: If you hardly knew him...

Japanese: 被害者が “イギリスかぶれ”だったコトを！
English: ...then why would you say that the victim was a "stuck-up British wannabe"!?

Japanese: ええと、それは‥‥
English: Um, well, you see...

Japanese: たしかに見たのですか？ ‥‥ユニオン・ジャックを。
English: Are you certain you saw the "Union Jack"?

Japanese: は‥‥はい。背中にでっかく 描いてありましたから。
English: Y-Yeah, I'm sure. It was right there on his back.

Japanese: 弁護人。 それがどうかしましたかな？
English: Ms. Fey, is there some point to this line of questioning?

Japanese: 裁判長。 この、現場写真を見てください。
English: Your Honor, please take another look at the crime scene photo.

Japanese: ‥‥ごらんのとおりです。 背中には、何も描かれていません！
English: As you can see, there's absolutely nothing written on the victim's back.

Japanese: でも！　これ、 革のジャケットじゃないですか！
English: Hey! Wait a minute! He's wearing a leather jacket!

Japanese: ユニオン・ジャックが描いて あったのは、シャツの背中‥‥
English: The Union Jack was on the back of the t-shirt he was wearing...

Japanese: あなたは、通りすがりに グーゼン、死体を見つけたはずよ。
English: I was under the impression that you accidentally came across the body.

Japanese: もし‥‥それが本当ならば、 知ってるはずがないでしょう？
English: But, if that was really the case, then you wouldn't know that, would you?

Japanese: 被害者が、このジャケットの下に 何を着ていたか、なんて！
English: You'd have no idea at all what he was wearing underneath that jacket!

Japanese: なるほどさん。あなた‥‥ この私に、ウソをついたのねっ！
English: Mr. Wright... You've been lying to me!

Japanese: ごめんなさわわあああああああん！
English: P-P-P-Please forgive me! Waaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!!

Japanese: こ、コラコラ！ 依頼人を泣かせてどうするんぢゃ！
English: Mia! You've made our client cry!

Japanese: いいんですっ！
English: Let him! That "P" on his chest doesn't stand for Phoenix anyways!

Japanese: わ‥‥私‥‥信じていたのに！ なるほどさんのコト‥‥
English: I can't believe I trusted him! Mr. Wright was all wrong!

Japanese: うふふふふふ‥‥ ここまでうまく行くとはねえ‥‥
English: Hee, hee, hee, hee. That was an impressive bit of cross-examination.

Japanese: これで弁護人は、ミゴトに立証 してくれたワケですな。
English: Thank you for uncovering the defendant's lies for me.

Japanese: 被告人が“現場を通りかかった” だけではないことを！
English: It's quite clear that this man did not simply "stumble upon" the scene of the crime!

Japanese: ううううう‥‥
English: Uggghhh...

Japanese: （しまった‥‥ 強く言いすぎたかしら‥‥）
English: (Uh oh... Did I go too far?)

Japanese: ところで、被告人。
English: By the way, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: カゼをひいておるようだが‥‥ 薬は飲まれないのかな？
English: You seem to have a rather bad cold. Have you taken any medicine for it?

Japanese: あ、あの。 飲んでいるんですけど‥‥
English: I, err, umm... Yeah, I took some but...

Japanese: 被告人が飲んでいる薬とは‥‥ 市販の《カゼゴロシ・Ｚ》ですな？
English: Was the medicine that you took an over-the-counter brand called "Coldkiller X"?

Japanese: そうなんですよ！ カゼ薬のケッ作ですからね！
English: Yeah, that's right! It kills colds good!

Japanese: ‥‥って、あれ。 どうして知ってるんですか？
English: Hey, wait a second...

Japanese: ボクが《カゼゴロシ・Ｚ》の 大ファンだ、ってコト‥‥
English: How did you know I'm a big fan of Coldkiller X...?

Japanese: うふふふふふ‥‥ところで、 その薬は‥‥今、持ってますかな？
English: Hee, hee, hee, hee... Would you happen to have that medicine with you right now?

Japanese: それが‥‥じつは、 どこかに落としちゃって‥‥
English: Well... Actually, I seem to have lost it somewhere...

Japanese: （カゼ薬を落とした‥‥？ 事件に関係あるのかしら‥‥）
English: (He lost it? Does this even have anything to do with the case...?)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: きみの薬が今、どこにあるか‥‥ 教えてあげよう。
English: Shall I tell you where your cold medicine is right now?
Huh...?

Japanese: 裁判長！　現場の写真を もう１枚、ごらんいただきたいッ！
English: Your Honor! I'd like you to take a look at another photo from the crime scene!

Japanese: こ‥‥これは！ ‥‥被害者の手の中に‥‥
English: Wh-What's this! In the victim's hand, it's...

Japanese: 《カゼゴロシ・Ｚ》‥‥！
English: It's Coldkiller X...!

Japanese: か、《カゼゴロシ・Ｚ》ぐらい、 私の部屋にだってありますっ！
English: Y-Yes, but even I've got a bottle of Coldkiller X in my apartment!

Japanese: ザンネンながら そうは行かないのですよ。
English: I'm sorry, but I'm afraid that argument won't work.

Japanese: この《カゼゴロシ・Ｚ》には‥‥ ハッキリ残っていたのです。
English: There's no doubt as to who this bottle of Coldkiller X belonged to.

Japanese: 被告人・成歩堂　龍一の 指紋がね！
English: Especially since Mr. Wright's fingerprints were all over it!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: おそらく‥‥殺意を 感じた呑田　菊三くんは‥‥
English: Sensing his murderous intent, Mr. Swallow must have picked up the bottle of medicine...

Japanese: 被告人が落とした薬ビンをそっと 拾って、手にかくしたのです。
English: ...dropped by Mr. Wright and hid it in his hand.

Japanese: 真犯人・成歩堂　龍一を 告発するためにッ！
English: His purpose in doing so can only have been to identify his killer as Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order in the court!

Japanese: 裁判長！　この写真と薬ビンを 証拠として提出いたします！
English: Your Honor! I'd like to present this photo and bottle as evidence!

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥受理しましょう。
English: Very well. The court will accept them into the record.

Japanese: 証拠品《現場写真・２》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Crime Photo 2 added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 証拠品《カゼゴロシ・Ｚ》のデータ を法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Coldkiller X added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ちなみに、被害者の腕時計は、 コショウしておりました。
English: Also, the victim's wristwatch was broken.

Japanese: コショウ‥‥ですか。
English: Broken...?

Japanese: 感電の際、強い電気のショックで 止まってしまったのです。
English: Yes, it ceased functioning when a large wave of electricity passed through it.

Japanese: さあ、被告人！ セツメイしてもらえますかな！
English: Well, Mr. Wright! Do you have some kind of explanation for all of this!?

Japanese: ううううう‥‥
English: Uggghhh...

Japanese: （サ、サイアクの事態ね‥‥）
English: (This is really bad...)

Japanese: ‥‥ううう‥‥シリが。 シリがああああ‥‥
English: Oh, my buttocks... My poor, poor hemorrhoids...

Japanese: ～本当にあったこと～
English: -- What Really Happened --

Japanese: じつは‥‥ボク、アイツに 呼び出されたんです。
English: The truth is... I went because he called me.

Japanese: アイツ、薬学部だから‥‥ その校舎の裏庭で、２時４５分に。
English: He was in the Pharmacology Dept., so we agreed to meet at 2:45 behind that building...

Japanese: 話をして‥‥３時ごろには、 もう別れました。
English: We talked for a bit, and then at around 3:00, we split up.

Japanese: それで、あとで戻ってきたら‥‥ アイツ、倒れていて。
English: Then later when I went back, I found him lying there.

Japanese: カゼゴロシ・Ｚは、この２・３日 ずっと飲んでいたんですけど‥‥
English: I'd been taking Coldkiller X for the last 2 or 3 days...

Japanese: 事件のあった日のおヒルごはんの ときから、なくなっちゃって。
English: But I lost my bottle of it around lunchtime on the day of the accident.

Japanese: 被告人！　さっきの証言と ゼンゼンちがうではないですかッ！
English: Mr. Wright! That's completely different than the testimony you gave previously!

Japanese: げほげほげほげほげほげほ。
English: *achoo* *achoo* *achoo*

Japanese: す、すみません！ 信じてもらえないと思って‥‥
English: I-I'm sorry, Your Honor! I was afraid you wouldn't believe the truth!

Japanese: そもそも、今の証言自体も きわめてウソっぽいようだがねえ。
English: You'll forgive me if I say I hardly find your current testimony any more credible.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Hmm... Ms. Fey, please begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: （おねがい、なるほどさん‥‥ これ以上、ウソはつかないで！）
English: (Oh please, Mr. Wright... Don't tell any more lies.)

Japanese: ～本当にあったこと～
English: -- What Really Happened --

Japanese: じつは‥‥ボク、アイツに 呼び出されたんです。
English: The truth is... I went because he called me.

Japanese: アイツ、薬学部だから‥‥ その校舎の裏庭で、２時４５分に。
English: He was in the Pharmacology Dept., so we agreed to meet at 2:45 behind that building...

Japanese: 話をして‥‥３時ごろには、 もう別れました。
English: We talked for a bit, and then at around 3:00, we split up.

Japanese: それで、あとで戻ってきたら‥‥ アイツ、倒れていて。
English: Then later when I went back, I found him lying there.

Japanese: カゼゴロシ・Ｚは、この２・３日 ずっと飲んでいたんですけど‥‥
English: I'd been taking Coldkiller X for the last 2 or 3 days...

Japanese: 事件のあった日のおヒルごはんの ときから、なくなっちゃって。
English: But I lost my bottle of it around lunchtime on the day of the accident.

Japanese: どうぢゃな？　千尋クン。
English: Well, Mia?

Japanese: 私‥‥どうしても 見つけられないんです。
English: I don't know...

Japanese: その‥‥ムジュンを。
English: I can't seem to find any contradictions.

Japanese: あのボウヤも、《恐怖のウソ人間》 というワケでもあるまい。
English: The boy isn't exactly what I'd call a natural-born liar, you know.

Japanese: そう毎回、デタラメを言われちゃ たまらんワイ。
English: But still, we can't have him continue to spout nonsense.

Japanese: でも‥‥じゃあ私、どうすれば！
English: I know... But what can I do!?

Japanese: あのボウヤ‥‥きっと、 まだ何かをかくしておる。
English: Well, I'm certain he must still be hiding something.

Japanese: ‥‥情報ぢゃ。今はとにかく、 情報を集めるんぢゃ！
English: Information! Right now it's information we need more than anything else!

Japanese: それまで、 被害者に会ったことは？
English: Had you ever met the victim before then?

Japanese: いえ！　ありませんよ！ ‥‥でも。
English: No! Never! But...

Japanese: あの日は、ちいちゃんのコトで 話があるから、って言われたから。
English: That day, he called me up and told me he wanted to talk about Dollie.

Japanese: “ちいちゃん”というのは？
English: And this "Dollie" person is...?

Japanese: ボクの‥‥ウンメイのヒト‥‥ えへへ。テレちゃうな、なんか。
English: My um... It's kind of embarrassing. She's my, umm, sweetheart...

Japanese: ぐはあッ！　‥‥なな、 なんぢゃ千尋クン！　イキナリ‥‥
English: Oomph...! Wh-What... What was that for, Mia!?

Japanese: ‥‥あ！　す、スミマセン！ 急に、殴りたくなっちゃって。
English: Oh! I'm so sorry! I just felt like slapping something all of a sudden!

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみさんは、 被害者・呑田くんの恋人でした。
English: Dahlia Hawthorne was also the lover of the murder victim, Doug Swallow.

Japanese: 彼女が、成歩堂くんと出会う 前のハナシですがね。
English: ...Before she met Mr. Wright, that is.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。キレイな三角関係、 というわけですか‥‥
English: Hmm... So it was one of those nasty love triangles, I see...

Japanese: ２時４５分というのは、 呑田　菊三さんの指定で？ 2:45?
English: Was it Mr. Swallow who indicated you should meet at

Japanese: ええ。時間ピッタリに会いました。
English: Yeah. And we were both there right on time.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。ところで、被害者は “薬学部”ということですが‥‥？
English: Hmm... You said the victim was in the Pharmacology Dept., correct?

Japanese: 医薬品の精製とか調合とか‥‥ そんなことを研究してるんです。
English: Yeah, he was studying how to manufacture and improve pharmaceuticals.

Japanese: 連中、『勇盟大学の錬金術師』 なんて呼ばれてますよ。
English: Everyone called him the "Alchemist of Ivy U".

Japanese: ほおほお。“れんきんじゅつ”‥‥
English: An "alchemist"... I see...

Japanese: とにかく、アヤしいんですよ。 実験室なんて、スゴいんだから！
English: I gotta admit it was a little suspicious. He had a whole laboratory and everything!

Japanese: いろんな化学薬品とか、 高圧電流を使った実験機械とか。
English: It was filled with chemicals and strange machines that run on high-voltage electricity.

Japanese: ほほ。それはおもしろい。 なんというか、ユメがありますな。
English: Ho ho. How fascinating. He sounds like he was quite an ambitious young man.

Japanese: （‥‥どうしよう？ 詳しく話を聞いてみようかな）
English: (What do I do!? Maybe I should ask him for some more details?)

Japanese: （‥‥必要なことは聞いたわ。 他の情報を集めましょう！）
English: (I've gotten everything I need from this statement. It's time to move on.)

Japanese: それでは、証言を つづけていただきましょうかな。
English: In any case, let's continue with your testimony.

Japanese: ２時４５分に会った‥‥というのは まちがいないのかしら？
English: So you are absolutely certain that you met at 2:45?

Japanese: ええ、たぶん。 授業が終わる時間なんですよ。
English: Yeah, pretty sure. That's the time class ends.

Japanese: ヤツらは実験してることが 多いから、あんまり関係ないけど。
English: But they're always doing experiments, so it doesn't matter much.

Japanese: 実験‥‥？
English: Experiments...?

Japanese: ええ。薬学部のヤツら、 いっつも実験ばっかりですよ。
English: Yeah. Those pharmacology guys are always in the lab whipping up something.

Japanese: （‥‥とにかく、時間に まちがいはないみたいね）
English: (Well, it looks like he's right about the time anyway.)

Japanese: それでは、証言を つづけていただきましょうかな。
English: Witness, let's go on with your testimony.

Japanese: 薬学部のこと‥‥ もう少し聞かせてくれるかしら。
English: I was wondering if you could tell us a bit more about the Pharmacology Department.

Japanese: いいですけど。 詳しいことは知りませんよ。
English: Well OK, sure. I don't know all that much though.

Japanese: さっき‥‥ あなたはこう言いましたね。
English: A little bit earlier in your testimony, you said something interesting.

Japanese: 彼らは“高圧電流を使った 実験機械”を使う‥‥と。
English: You said the department uses strange machines that run on high-voltage electricity.

Japanese: そうみたいですよ。 アブないハナシですよね！
English: That's right. And they sure look dangerous.

Japanese: 規格より高い電力を使うから、 専用の高圧電線を引いていて‥‥
English: They use non-standard voltages, so there are high- voltage cables everywhere.

Japanese: “高圧電線”‥‥
English: "High-voltage cables"...

Japanese: ええ。校舎内に 電柱が特設してあるんです。
English: Yeah, there were electrical poles set up all around the building.

Japanese: 高圧電線なら、校舎にそって、 アタマの上を走ってますよ。
English: The high-voltage cables run overhead around the roof.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら‥‥やっと、 話が見えてきたわね‥‥）
English: (Finally I think we're getting somewhere...!)

Japanese: なんの話をしたのですか？
English: So what was it you were talking about?

Japanese: それは‥‥その。げほげほ。 いずれまたいつか、次の機会に。
English: You know... *achoo* *cough* That maybe we should hang out again sometime.

Japanese: （“次の機会”なんてものが ホントにあればいいけど‥‥）
English: ("Hang out again sometime"? I wish that were true.)

Japanese: “戻ってきた”というのは‥‥？
English: So you say you went back...

Japanese: あの‥‥だから、 死体が発見された現場です。
English: Umm, yeah. That's when I found the body.

Japanese: なぜ、戻ってきたの？ あなた、怒っていたんでしょう？
English: Yes, but why did you go back in the first place? Weren't you angry with him?

Japanese: え‥‥！ そ、そうなんですけど。
English: Well, th-that's right, I was.

Japanese: なるほどさん！　どうしてなの？
English: Then why, Mr. Wright? Why did you go back there?

Japanese: ‥‥その。やっぱり、仲なおり しようかな、って思ったんです。
English: Umm... I thought maybe we could make up.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥へっくし。
English: *achoo* *achoo*

Japanese: （このヒンヤリした空気‥‥ダレも 信用していないみたいね）
English: (Judging by the atmosphere... I'm pretty sure no one is buying this.)

Japanese: この時期にカゼをひくなんて めずらしいですね。
English: It's rather unusual to catch a cold this time of the year, isn't it?

Japanese: ええ。あたたかくなってきたから、 ちょっと油断しちゃって。
English: Yeah, I always get a little careless when the weather starts to warm up.

Japanese: やっぱり、おなかを出して 寝ちゃ、ダメですよねえ。
English: I guess I shouldn't sleep with the window open this early in spring, huh?

Japanese: （コドモじゃないんだから‥‥）
English: (I suppose common sense is not always common...)

Japanese: それで‥‥あなたが、そのカゼ薬を 飲んでいたことを知っていたのは？
English: So... Did anyone else know that you were taking cold medicine?

Japanese: ‥‥さあ。食後に、 いつも飲んでいたからなあ。
English: Well, I always took one after meals...

Japanese: ボクのトモダチなら、みんな 知っていたと思いますけど。
English: So I'm pretty sure all my friends knew about it.

Japanese: “事件があった日”ですか。 そのとき、昼食は、どなたと‥‥？
English: On the day of the incident, what did you do for lunch...?

Japanese: え？　そんなコト、 事件にカンケイあるんですか？
English: Huh? What does that have to do with anything?

Japanese: ‥‥もしかしたらね。
English: You can never be too sure...

Japanese: おヒルはいつも、ちいちゃんと ２人っきりで食べるんですよ。
English: I always eat with Dollie... Just the two of us.

Japanese: ちいちゃんのおベントウ、 とーってもオイシイんですよ。
English: Dollie's homemade lunches are just the greatest!

Japanese: もお、タマゴ焼きなんて、 ほっぺが落っこちそうで。へへへ。
English: Mmm! Her mini-omelettes are magically delicious! Eheh heh heh.

Japanese: ぐはあッ！　なな、 なぜ、ワシのほっぺを‥‥！
English: Yeowch!! Wh-Why did you punch me in the jaw!?

Japanese: ‥‥あ！　す、スミマセン！ 急に、ビンタしたくなっちゃって。
English: Oh! I-I'm so sorry...! I just felt like hurting someone all of a sudden!

Japanese: ‥‥もう、じゅうぶんでしょう。
English: I think that's enough for now.

Japanese: 被告人と被害者が会ったのは、 死亡推定時刻のころです。
English: So the defendant and the victim met at approximately the time of his death.

Japanese: さらに、立ち去ったはずの被告人は なぜか、現場に戻っている‥‥
English: And then the defendant returned to the scene for some unknown reason.

Japanese: 薬ビンの説明も、ナットクできる ものではありませんでしたな。
English: I'm not entirely convinced by his explanation about the medicine bottle either.

Japanese: ハッキリ言いましょう。
English: Let me be frank here, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥被告人。 あなたの証言は、信用できません。
English: Your testimony cannot be trusted.

Japanese: そ‥‥そんな！
English: Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: うふふふふふ‥‥
English: Hee, hee, hee, hee...

Japanese: やはり、おじょうさんには 荷が重かったようですな‥‥
English: I knew it was too much work for a little girl.
Hmph...!

Japanese: ‥‥ただし。まだ１つだけ、 問題が残っています。
English: However, there is one mystery that still remains.

Japanese: モンダイ‥‥ですと？
English: There is, Your Honor?

Japanese: モチロン、殺害方法です。
English: How the murder was carried out, of course.

Japanese: 被害者が、どうやって “感電死”したのか？
English: Just how was the victim electrocuted?

Japanese: ‥‥たしか、凶器はまだ 提出されていませんでしたな？
English: I don't believe the murder weapon has been produced yet, correct?

Japanese: まあ、それはそうですな。 ‥‥たしかに。
English: Well, that is... I... You are correct, Your Honor.

Japanese: （被害者の呑田　菊三が “どうやって死んだか”‥‥？）
English: (So how exactly was Mr. Swallow killed...?)

Japanese: （もし、その方法を立証することが できれば‥‥）
English: (If I could somehow establish how it was done...)

Japanese: （このピンチを切り抜けることが できるかもしれない‥‥！）
English: (Maybe I could still come out of this mess smelling like a rose!)

Japanese: （１つだけ、考えはある。 でも‥‥立証はムリよ‥‥！）
English: (Hmm, I do have one idea, but it would be impossible to prove it.)

Japanese: いいかの、千尋クン。
English: Mia, m'dear.

Japanese: 何か考えがあるのならば、 とにかく発言しておくコトぢゃ。
English: If you have any ideas, now is the time to present them.

Japanese: ‥‥先生！
English: Mr. Grossberg!

Japanese: チンモクは何も生まん。 ‥‥それが法廷ぢゃ。
English: Remember, silence is NOT golden. ...In court, anyway.

Japanese: （チンモクは何も生まない‥‥ それならば！）
English: (Silence is not golden, huh? Well in that case...)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: な、なんですかな、弁護人。
English: Y-Yes, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: これまでの証言を総合すれば‥‥ 答えを出すことができるはずです！
English: I believe that if we were to piece together everything we've heard up until now...

Japanese: どうして被害者は死んだのか‥‥？
English: ...we should be able to solve the mystery of how Mr. Swallow died.

Japanese: そ、そうなのですか！
English: Th-That would be most impressive!

Japanese: うふふふふふ‥‥ これは、大きく出ましたなあ。
English: Hee, hee, hee, hee... Quite the brash statement coming from a rookie!

Japanese: モチロン‥‥新人のあなたも 法廷のルールはご存じでしょうな？
English: But even a beginner like you must understand the basic rules of the court, yes?

Japanese: ワレワレの武器は、つねに “証拠品”であることを！
English: An attorney must be able to substantiate their arguments with evidence!

Japanese: も、モチロンわかってますっ！ （ちょっぴり、忘れてたけど‥‥）
English: Hmph! Of course I know that! (Actually, I had totally forgotten about that...)

Japanese: それでは、弁護人。 提示していただきましょう。
English: Now then, Ms. Fey. Let me see what you've got.

Japanese: 被害者が“感電死”した 原因を示す証拠品を‥‥！
English: Show me how you believe the victim was electrocuted!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたには ガッカリしましたな、弁護人。
English: Ms. Fey, I'm deeply disappointed in you.

Japanese: ‥‥私も ガッカリしましたぞ、弁護人。
English: I'm disappointed as well, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥ワシもちょっと ガッカリしちまったぞ、千尋クン。
English: To be honest, I'm a bit disappointed too, Mia.
*achoo*

Japanese: （も、もう一度よ、千尋！ ‥‥よく、考えるの！）
English: (Argh! I need to use my brain on this!)

Japanese: （こんなところでイキナリ 負けちゃ、シャレにならないわ）
English: (Come on, Mia! You can't strike out here!)

Japanese: 被害者が亡くなった、 その“原因”‥‥
English: As for the cause of death...

Japanese: この現場写真に、しっかり 写ってるじゃないですか！
English: ...I'd say this picture captures it quite well!

Japanese: な、なんですとッ！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: ‥‥特に、凶器になりそうな モノは、見あたりませんが‥‥
English: But there is nothing that even remotely resembles a murder weapon here...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥それでは、弁護人。 教えていただけますかな。
English: Hmm, I'm afraid the defense is going to have to explain this in a bit more detail.

Japanese: この写真のどこに、 “凶器”があるのか‥‥？
English: Ms. Fey, where exactly in this photo is the murder weapon?

Japanese: もちろん‥‥ここらへんですっ！ ‥‥たぶん。
English: Well naturally... it's right around here! ...I think.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたには ガッカリしましたな、弁護人。
English: Ms. Fey, I'm deeply disappointed in you.

Japanese: ‥‥私も ガッカリしましたぞ、弁護人。
English: I'm disappointed as well, Ms. Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥ワシもちょっと ガッカリしちまったぞ、千尋クン。
English: To be honest, I'm a bit disappointed too, Mia.
*achoo*

Japanese: （も、もう一度よ、千尋！ ‥‥よく、考えるの！）
English: (Argh! I need to use my brain on this!)

Japanese: （こんなところでイキナリ 負けちゃ、シャレにならないわ）
English: (Come on, Mia! You can't strike out here!)

Japanese: もちろん‥‥ここですっ！
English: Well, naturally... it's right here!

Japanese: それは‥‥なんですかな？
English: That's... That's... What is that?

Japanese: 切れた送電線‥‥でしょう。
English: A severed electrical cable, I believe... Your Honor.

Japanese: 証言を思い出してください。
English: Remember the testimony we've heard.

Japanese: 薬学部では、実験に 高圧電流を使用するため‥‥
English: The machines the pharmacology students use in their experi- ments require high voltage...

Japanese: 特殊な送電線が 引かれているのです！
English: ...and because of that, there are special high-voltage cables strung up everywhere!

Japanese: 高圧‥‥電流‥‥！
English: So then, the high-voltage cable...!

Japanese: この送電線こそが、被害者の死因。 そう考えるのが自然です！
English: Yes, the high-voltage cable is the cause of death. That is the most likely explanation!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥た、たしかに‥‥ そうかもしれませんな。
English: Hmm... That certainly sounds plausible.

Japanese: 亜内検事！　いかがですか？
English: Well, Mr. Payne? What do you have to say about this?

Japanese: まあ‥‥よくやった、と ホメてさし上げましょうかな。
English: Well... I believe some praise is in order.

Japanese: なんですって‥‥
English: Don't toy with me, old man!

Japanese: 被害者の死因は、 その送電線かもしれない。
English: Now, now. The victim's cause of death may indeed have been a high-voltage cable.

Japanese: しかし！　それが何を イミするか、考えていただきたい！
English: However! I want you to think about what that really implies!

Japanese: 切れた送電線を“凶器”として 使うことができたのは‥‥
English: The only one who had the opportunity to use the cable as a murder weapon was...

Japanese: やはり、被告人ではないですか！
English: The defendant!

Japanese: げほ！
English: *achoo*

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 そのとおりですな。
English: Hmm... That much is certainly true.

Japanese: そして、最後にもう１つ。 ワレワレには証拠があるのですよ。
English: Yes, and that's not all. We have proof.

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一の犯行を立証する、 決定的な証拠がね！
English: Irrefutable proof that will establish that Mr. Wright was the murderer.

Japanese: そ、その証拠とは‥‥ いったい、どんな！
English: Y-You do!? Well, what is it!?

Japanese: ‥‥指紋ですよ。
English: His fingerprints.

Japanese: しもん‥‥？
English: Fingerprints...?

Japanese: この薬ビン以外にも、被告人は まだ、指紋を残していたのですか！
English: You mean that the defendant's fingerprints were on something besides the medicine bottle?

Japanese: 現場写真を見ていただきましょう。
English: Let's take another look at the crime scene photo.

Japanese: ごらんのとおり、被害者は 革のジャケットを着ています。
English: As you can see, the victim is wearing a leather jacket.

Japanese: 革には‥‥そう。 指紋が残りますよねえ。
English: And as you may know, leather holds fingerprints quite well.

Japanese: あっ！ ‥‥ま、まさか‥‥？
English: Ah! Y-You mean...?

Japanese: ジャケットのムネの部分に‥‥ ハッキリ残っていたのですよ。
English: Yes. It was quite clearly imprinted on the chest area of the victim's jacket.

Japanese: 被告人の手のひらの跡が、 キレイに‥‥ね！
English: The palm print of the defendant's very own hand!

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: Wh-Whaaat!?

Japanese: そんなところに指紋がつくケースは １つしか考えられない。
English: I can only think of one way Mr. Wright could have left a print like that.

Japanese: 殺意を持って、切れた送電線に 向かってつきとばしたときです！
English: Intent on murder, he squarely pushed the victim towards the severed electrical cable!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ もう、けっこうです！
English: Order! Order! Order! That's enough!

Japanese: ‥‥これで、ギモンの余地は カンゼンになくなりました。
English: I think we can conclude that there is no reason to continue with this cross-examination.

Japanese: ‥‥オシマイ、ぢゃな。
English: Stick a fork in us, we're done.

Japanese: せ‥‥先生！
English: M-Mr. Grossberg!

Japanese: シリの痛みでわかる。 ‥‥あきらめるんぢゃ、千尋クン。
English: My hemorrhoids never lie. The show is over, Mia.

Japanese: あのボウヤは‥‥ 最初から、有罪だったんぢゃよ。
English: I knew that boy was guilty the first time I saw him.

Japanese: ちがう！　ちがう‥‥！
English: N-No! You're wrong!

Japanese: なるほどさんは‥‥ ゼッタイに無実なんです！
English: Mr. Wright is innocent.

Japanese: ‥‥本法廷は、これ以上の 審理を必要としません！
English: No further evidence is required to convince me of this man's guilt!

Japanese: さ‥‥裁判長‥‥！
English: Y-Your Honor...!

Japanese: 被告人・成歩堂　龍一に、 この場で判決を言いわたします！
English: At this time, I am prepared to render a verdict in this case!

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、 何かあるのですか？　弁護人。
English: Do you have something further to add, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: これでいいの？　なるほどくん！
English: Is this what you want, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: あなたはまだ、本当のことを ‥‥真実を話していない。
English: You still haven't told us the truth... the whole truth.

Japanese: それなのに‥‥今、 判決が下ろうとしているのよ！
English: If you don't say something now, the judge is going to hand down his verdict!

Japanese: で、でも‥‥ボク。 い、言えませんっ！
English: B-But I... I can't! I-I just can't say it!

Japanese: ホントのコトを言っちゃったら、 それこそ‥‥
English: If I told you what really happened, then I'd b-be...

Japanese: 大丈夫‥‥なるほどくん。 私を信じて。
English: It's OK, Mr. Wright. I'm your attorney. You can trust me.

Japanese: ち‥‥千尋さん‥‥
English: M-Miss Fey...

Japanese: あなたがどんな証言を しようと‥‥
English: No matter what it is you have to say...

Japanese: 私はあなたを信じる。 最後まで、あなたを弁護するわ。
English: I believe in you and I'll represent you to the very end.

Japanese: ‥‥ワレワレは、すでに 被告人の有罪を立証しました。
English: We've already established the defendant's guilt.

Japanese: 彼のコトバは すでに、必要ありません！
English: There's no further need for him to say anything.

Japanese: げほッ！
English: *cough* *cough*

Japanese: ま‥‥待ってくださいっ！
English: W-Wait a minute!

Japanese: なるほどくん‥‥
English: Mr. Wright...

Japanese: ボク‥‥ ホントのコトを言います！ I...
English: I'll tell you what really happened!

Japanese: だから、成歩堂くん。 もう、その必要は‥‥
English: But I've already told you, Mr. Wright. There's no need for further...

Japanese: げほッ！
English: *cough*

Japanese: ボクが‥‥ボクが、 つきとばしたんです！
English: I-I-I... I did it... I admit it! I pushed him!

Japanese: アイツを‥‥呑田　菊三を！
English: It's my fault... My f-fault that D-Doug Swallow is dead!

Japanese: ‥‥彼女とは、 もう会わないほうがいい。
English: That girl... You shouldn't see her anymore.

Japanese: そんなコト、 きみに言われたくないよ！
English: Hey! It's none of your business!

Japanese: ‥‥アンタのためなんだ。 きっと、よくないことが起こる。
English: I'm telling you for your sake. If you continue to see her, it's going to be bad news.

Japanese: う、ウソだッ！
English: Y-You're lying!

Japanese: いいか、聞くんだ。 ‥‥あの女はなぁ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥
English: Just listen to me. There's something you need to know about that girl... ...

Japanese: ‥‥やめてくれッ！
English: Stop it!

Japanese: 彼女のコト‥‥ そんなふうに言うなッ！
English: D-Don't talk about her like that!

Japanese: 今の発言‥‥ホンキですかッ！
English: What you just said... Was that the truth!?

Japanese: ボク‥‥コワかったんです。 ホントのコトを話したら‥‥
English: Y-Yes, I... I was afraid... Afraid that if I told the truth...

Japanese: ダレが考えたって、 ぼくが犯人になっちまう！
English: ...everyone would think I was the murderer for sure!

Japanese: ‥‥今の時点ですでに、ダレが 考えても犯人はあなたでしょう。
English: Well, as things currently stand, we're all absolutely convinced you are!

Japanese: ‥‥おねがいします！
English: P-Please...!

Japanese: もう一度だけ、 チャンスをください！
English: Please give me one more chance to explain!

Japanese: ぼく、今度こそ‥‥ 真実を証言したいんです！
English: This time I swear... I swear I'll tell the whole truth!

Japanese: 大丈夫だよね、千尋さん！ ぼく‥‥信じてるから！
English: It'll be OK, won't it, Miss Fey? I... I believe in you!

Japanese: え！　‥‥どうもありがと。
English: Oh! Um, th-thank you.

Japanese: （まさか‥‥ホントに つきとばしていたなんて‥‥）
English: (I still can't believe it... He really did push the victim...)

Japanese: ワシのシリが‥‥暴れだしておる！ 暴れだしておるぞおおおお‥‥
English: Unnngh... It feels like my hemorrhoids are doing the Harlem Shake!

Japanese: ～被害者をつきとばしたこと～
English: -- When I Pushed The Victim --

Japanese: アイツが‥‥ちいちゃんのコト、 悪く言ったから‥‥
English: That guy... He was talking bad about Dollie...

Japanese: ついカッとなって つきとばしちゃったんです！
English: I lost my temper and gave him a shove!

Japanese: そのとき、なんか大きな 音がしたような気がするけど‥‥
English: At that moment, I heard some kind of loud noise...

Japanese: 立ち去ってから少しして、 ちょっと心配になったんです。
English: A little while after I left, I started to get worried.

Japanese: それで、戻ってみたら‥‥ あ、アイツ‥‥し、死んでいて！
English: So I went back. But h-he was just laying there, d-dead!

Japanese: ‥‥ハナシはカンタンですな。
English: Well, the explanation is really quite simple.

Japanese: つきとばしたとき、呑田くんは 切れた送電線に触れてしまった。
English: When you pushed him, Mr. Swallow flew back and touched the electrical cable.

Japanese: そのショックで、彼は死んだ。 ‥‥それだけのことです。
English: He died from the shock. And that, as they say, is that.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥なるほど‥‥
English: Hmm... A simple explanation indeed.

Japanese: 事件のあったころ、現場には 小雨がふっていました。
English: At the time of the incident, a light rain had been falling.

Japanese: 雨にぬれた被害者は、電気を 通しやすい状態にあったのです！
English: Wet from the rain, the victim was more easily electrocuted.

Japanese: で‥‥でも！
English: B-But!

Japanese: つきとばしたとき、送電線なんて たれていませんでしたよ！
English: When I pushed him, there weren't any electrical cables nearby!

Japanese: 現場にそんなものがあったら、 いくらぼくでも、気がつきます！
English: If there had been something like that, even I would've noticed it!

Japanese: （そうよね‥‥。いくら なるほどくんでも気づくわ！）
English: (That's true... Even a dufus like him couldn't miss that!)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ 弁護人、よろしいですか。
English: Hmm... Ms. Fey.

Japanese: 今回の尋問で新しい事実が 浮かばなかった場合‥‥
English: Let me warn you right now that if your cross-examination doesn't yield any new facts...

Japanese: ただちに判決を言いわたします。 ‥‥そのつもりで。
English: ...I intend to deliver my verdict without further delay. Are we clear on that?

Japanese: ‥‥は、はい。
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （あきらめちゃダメよ、千尋！）
English: (Don't give up, Mia!)

Japanese: （なるほどくんが無実ならば‥‥ 手がかりは必ず、あるわ！）
English: (If he is innocent, there must be some kind of evidence somewhere that will prove it!)

Japanese: ～被害者をつきとばしたこと～
English: -- When I Pushed The Victim --

Japanese: アイツが‥‥ちいちゃんのコト、 悪く言ったから‥‥
English: That guy... He was talking bad about Dollie...

Japanese: ついカッとなって つきとばしちゃったんです！
English: I lost my temper and gave him a shove!

Japanese: そのとき、なんか大きな 音がしたような気がするけど‥‥
English: At that moment, I heard some kind of loud noise...

Japanese: 立ち去ってから少しして、 ちょっと心配になったんです。
English: A little while after I left, I started to get worried.

Japanese: それで、戻ってみたら‥‥ あ、アイツ‥‥し、死んでいて！
English: So I went back. But h-he was just laying there, d-dead!

Japanese: この証言から、彼の無実を示す 可能性を見つけぬかぎり‥‥
English: Unless we can find something that shows his innocence from that testimony, m'dear...

Japanese: 今度こそ、判決は決定的なモノに なるんぢゃぞ！
English: I'm afraid the judge will make his final decision with no remorse whatsoever!

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Y-Yes, sir!

Japanese: （とにかく、情報がほしい‥‥ ムジュンを探すための情報が！）
English: (Right now I need more info; info that will help me turn up some contradictions!)

Japanese: つきとばしたら、アイツ‥‥ カサを下じきにして倒れました。
English: After I shoved him, he... He fell down on top of his cheap umbrella.

Japanese: 呑田さん、 何を言ったのかしら？
English: So what kinds of things did Mr. Swallow say to you?

Japanese: アイツ、ヒドいコトを 言ったんですよ！
English: He said all sorts of terrible things about Dollie!

Japanese: “あの子はワルい子だ”って！
English: He said that she was a bad girl!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。それだけですかな？
English: Umm... Is that all?

Japanese: はい！
English: Yup!

Japanese: ‥‥だそうです。弁護人。
English: Well, Ms. Fey, you heard him yourself.

Japanese: （なるほどくん‥‥おねがい！）
English: (Oh boy! You're not doing yourself any favors here, Mr. Wright!)

Japanese: （これ以上、私に ハジをかかせないで‥‥）
English: (Please don't make this harder for me than it already is!)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥そんなコトを 言われたから、ボクは‥‥
English: Anyway... After he said that, I just, I just...

Japanese: そのときのこと、もう少し 詳しく教えてもらえるかしら？
English: Can you tell me about what happened in a little more detail?

Japanese: アイツ‥‥一方的に 言いたいコト言って‥‥
English: That guy... He just said what he wanted to say to me...

Japanese: 手に持ったジャケットを 着て、帰ろうとしたんです。
English: And then he put on the jacket he was holding and started to leave.

Japanese: だから、ボク‥‥ アタマに血がのぼっちゃって。
English: That's when... That's when I lost my temper and flew into a furious frenzy!

Japanese: そおっと、かるーく押したんです。 ‥‥アイツのムネを。
English: I just gave him a... light, gentle shove to the chest.

Japanese: それで、そのとき‥‥ 切れた送電線は、なかったのね？
English: And when you did that, there was no severed cable anywhere to be seen?

Japanese: はいッ！　そんなもの、 ゼッタイにありませんでした！
English: Right! There was nothing like that at all!

Japanese: ‥‥見落としたのかも しれませんぞ？
English: But is it possible that you merely overlooked it?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥まあ。たしかに そうかもしれませんケド。
English: Well, I guess it's possible.

Japanese: （アッサリ認めないでよ！）
English: (What are you doing!? Don't let that guy steamroll over you like cheap asphalt!)

Japanese: つきとばした瞬間のこと‥‥ これは、かなり重要です。
English: I believe what's important here is the moment the push occurred.

Japanese: 証言をつづけてもらいましょうか。
English: Let's continue on with the testimony, witness.

Japanese: “大きな音”‥‥ それは、どんな？
English: A "loud noise"? And what would you say that loud noise was, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: よくわからないんですけど‥‥ かなり大きな音でしたよ。
English: I'm not sure... But it was really loud!

Japanese: “バチッ！”‥‥みたいな。 あれ、なんの音だったのかなあ。
English: It was like, "SNAP!"... You know, come to think of it, I wonder what that was...

Japanese: うふふふふふ‥‥
English: Hee, hee, hee, hee...

Japanese: 被害者が感電したときの音‥‥ それしか考えられないでしょう。
English: Clearly, Your Honor, it was the sound of the victim being electrocuted.

Japanese: ‥‥き、 決めつけないでくださいっ！
English: Y-You're not qualified to decide that!

Japanese: （どうしようかな？　ヘタに つっこむのもキケンかしら‥‥）
English: (What should I do? I'm treading on some dangerous ground here...)

Japanese: （今は、もっと他に 聞くことがあるような気がする）
English: (I've got other, more pressing things to ask about.)

Japanese: ‥‥けっこうです。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Very well, Mr. Wright, let's move on, shall we?

Japanese: なるほどくん。その“音”‥‥ とても大事なコトかもしれないわ。
English: Mr. Wright, that "loud noise" you heard may be extremely important.

Japanese: よく思い出してみて！
English: So try to remember what it was!

Japanese: ううう‥‥なんていうか、 鋭い音だったんですよ。
English: Umm, how do I put it... It was like a sharp crack.

Japanese: あ！　もしかして、あの音かな？
English: Aha! Could it... Could it have been...?

Japanese: な、なに！　言いなさい！　早く！
English: Yes? Could it have been? Hurry up and tell us!

Japanese: つきとばしたとき、アイツ 持っていたカサを落としたんです。
English: When I pushed him, he dropped the umbrella he was holding.

Japanese: アイツ、カサの上に倒れて‥‥ コワしちゃったんです。
English: He fell right on top of it, and it broke.

Japanese: そのときの音だったのかも‥‥。
English: That was probably the noise I heard.

Japanese: カサ、ねえ‥‥。それは、 被害者のものですか？
English: An umbrella, huh? And did that umbrella belong to the victim?

Japanese: ええ。アイツにお似合いの、 安っぽいビニールのカサですよ！
English: Yeah, it was a plastic umbrella. Cheap and frail, kind of like the owner!

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、ボクはそれすら持って なくて、ビショぬれでしたけど。
English: Then again, I wish I had any kind of umbrella. I was totally soaked to the bone.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥弁護人。
English: Hmm... Ms. Fey.

Japanese: いかがですか？　今の証言、 何か重要なことなのですかな？
English: What do you think? Is there something important in that testimony just now?

Japanese: え！　ええと、それは‥‥
English: Umm, well...

Japanese: モチロン、イミなんて ありません！
English: Of course it's not important!

Japanese: べべ弁護人。‥‥ムネを張って 言わないように！
English: M-Ms. Fey! How can you state that so proudly!?

Japanese: ええ、ありませんとも！ イミなんて。もうゼンゼン。
English: Well, it just isn't! It's not important one bit! Not even one iota. OK?

Japanese: コラコラ！　千尋クン、 ヤケになっちゃイカン！
English: Mia! That's enough! You're making a fool of yourself!

Japanese: あの‥‥。 つづけていいですか？
English: Umm... Is it OK if I go on?

Japanese: （これこそ、私の待っていた 新しい情報じゃない、千尋！）
English: (This is it, Mia! The new information you've been waiting for!)

Japanese: その安っぽいカサのコト‥‥ モチロン重要です！
English: Of course it's important! No! This cheap umbrella is more than important; it's vital!

Japanese: 正式に、証言に加えてください！
English: I want to officially have it entered into the testimony!

Japanese: ハッ！　安っぽい弁護士には 安モノの情報が似合うようですな。
English: Ha! How perfectly fitting. Flimsy information for a flimsy lawyer!

Japanese: 弁護人の要請を 受け入れましょう。‥‥証人。
English: The court agrees to the defense's request.

Japanese: 安っぽいカサのことを 証言に加えてください！
English: Witness, please add the bit about the cheap umbrella to your testimony.

Japanese: 被害者をつきとばして‥‥ すぐに現場を立ち去ったの？
English: After you shoved the victim, did you leave the scene right away?

Japanese: ハイ！　だってボク、 ハラが立ったから！
English: Yes, I did! I admit it! I-I was furious!

Japanese: 倒れた被害者‥‥呑田くんが どうなったか、たしかめずに？
English: You left without even checking Mr. Swallow's condition?

Japanese: え、ええ。‥‥だから、シンパイに なったんですけど。
English: Well, um, yeah... But like I said, I got worried about him later.

Japanese: そのとき‥‥現場には、 他にだれもいなかったのかしら？
English: At that time, did you see anyone else at the scene of the crime?

Japanese: げほ。‥‥げほげほげほげほ。 ‥‥ええ。いませんでした。
English: *cough* *cough*... *cough* *cough* *cough* Um, nope. Nobody...

Japanese: （どうにも挙動不審なのよね‥‥ あのセキっぷり）
English: (Geez. Could that stupid cough possibly sound any phonier?)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: そうなるとやはり、犯人は 他に考えにくいですなあ‥‥
English: In that case, it's very hard to believe someone else could have been the murderer...

Japanese: カサの上に、呑田さんが倒れた。 まちがいないかしら？
English: So Mr. Swallow fell on top of his umbrella? And you are certain of this?

Japanese: ええ。ちゃんと カラダの下にありましたよ。
English: Yeah. It was right there under him.

Japanese: ‥‥その。そうじゃなかったら、 ちょっと借りちゃおかな、って。
English: Actually, if it hadn't been under him, I was planning on borrowing it for myself.

Japanese: そのカサを？
English: The umbrella, you mean?

Japanese: あのときも、このセーターを 着てたんですけど。
English: Well yeah, you see I was wearing this sweater here...

Japanese: これ、ちいちゃんが編んで くれたんですよ。夜なべして。
English: Dollie stayed up late for nights at a time knitting it for me...

Japanese: ボクたちのアイを、 ぬらしたくなくて‥‥
English: I didn't want the rain to dampen the handmade symbol of her love...

Japanese: ぐはあッ！　‥‥だから、 ワシのハラをけるなッ！
English: Oomph! My stomach is not to be used as your personal soccer ball, Mia!

Japanese: ‥‥あ！　す、スミマセン！
English: Ah! I-I'm so sorry!

Japanese: それでは、証人。 証言をつづけてください。
English: Continue on with your testimony, witness.

Japanese: ‥‥どうして最初から この証言をしてくれなかったの？
English: Why didn't you testify about the umbrella from the very beginning?

Japanese: だって‥‥ こんなコト言ったら‥‥
English: Come on... If I had mentioned that...

Japanese: もっと早く 反撃ができていたのに。
English: I would've been able to counter the prosecution's arguments earlier!

Japanese: ど、どういうことですかな？
English: Wh-What do you mean by that?

Japanese: 現場写真を見てください！
English: Take another look at the crime scene photo!

Japanese: 被害者は、カサの上に倒れた‥‥ 証人は、そう証言しました。
English: According to Mr. Wright, the victim fell on top of his umbrella.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし、この写真では‥‥
English: However, if you look closely...

Japanese: カサは、死体から離れた 電柱のところにあります！
English: ...the umbrella is nowhere near the victim. Actually, it's by the electrical pole!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに！
English: Y-You're absolutely right!

Japanese: 結論はアキラカです。 被告人が立ち去ったあと‥‥
English: The conclusion here is obvious.

Japanese: 被害者は、その場所から 移動した。‥‥つまり！
English: After the defendant left, the victim moved from where he fell. In other words...

Japanese: 被告人につきとばされたとき、 呑田さんは、死んでいなかった！
English: After he was pushed by the defendant, Mr. Swallow was still alive!

Japanese: バカなあああああぁぁぁっ！
English: Nnnnooooo!!

Japanese: ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ 被害者が‥‥移動した‥‥！
English: Order! Order! Order! The victim... He moved...!?

Japanese: 亜内検事！　カサは‥‥ この写真のカサは、どこにッ？
English: Mr. Payne! The umbrella in this photo... Where is it now?

Japanese: い、いちおう 回収してありますが‥‥
English: W-Well, it was collected by the police at the crime scene...

Japanese: 今すぐ、証拠として 提出していただきましょう！
English: I want it presented as evidence immediately!

Japanese: 証拠品《カサ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Umbrella added to the Court Record.

Japanese: しかし‥‥そんなカサごとき！ 風で‥‥風で飛ばされただけだッ！
English: B-But the umbrella could have simply been blown there by the wind!

Japanese: 証言によれば、被害者は カサの上に倒れたはずです。
English: According to the testimony, the victim fell on top of the umbrella.

Japanese: 風で飛ばされるなんて、 考えられません！
English: There's simply no way it could have been blown there by the wind!

Japanese: むぐぐぐぅぅぅ‥‥ッ！ で、でも！　でもでも
English: Nnn... Gggg... Hrrmph! B-B-But...!

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに‥‥カサの件は ささいなコトに見えます。
English: I know this matter of the umbrella seems relatively trivial...

Japanese: しかし！　ギモンが残るかぎり、 判決を下すことはできません！
English: But as long as the smallest doubt remains, I cannot render final judgment!

Japanese: なぎゃらわああああああああッ！
English: Noooooooooooooooooooooo!!!

Japanese: ‥‥信じられません。
English: However, I still find it hard to believe that a huge hole has been blown in the...

Japanese: 有罪を確定すると思われた証言に、 まさかこんな“穴”があったとは！
English: ...prosecution's case by the defendant's testimony.

Japanese: 《成歩堂の証言書》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Phoenix's Testimony added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ち‥‥千尋クン！　やりおったな！
English: Well done, Mia!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥うふ。 うふふふふふふふふふふふふふふふ
English: ......Hee hee. Hee hee hee hee hee...

Japanese: なな、なんですか、亜内検事。 その、ほがらかな笑い声は‥‥
English: Mr. Payne! What are you chuckling about?

Japanese: さすがに‥‥ ムシがよすぎましたかな。
English: Pardon me, Your Honor. It seems I was expecting too much of a free ride.

Japanese: 被告人の尋問だけで 有罪判決をいただこう、などと‥‥
English: It was foolish to think I could establish guilt through cross-examination alone.

Japanese: な、なんですと！
English: I'm afraid I don't follow what you're saying!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥“次の証人”が いるみたいですね。
English: Let me guess. You have another witness.

Japanese: さよう！　しかも、 “決定的な”証人がねえ。
English: Exactly! And this witness's testimony will be incontrovertible!

Japanese: ど、どなたですかッ！
English: Well!? Who is this witness!?

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみさんです。
English: Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: みやなぎ　ちなみ？　もしかして ‥‥あの。ちいちゃん、ですかな？
English: Dahlia Hawthorne? You don't mean... "Dollie"?

Japanese: そのとおりです。
English: I do, Your Honor.

Japanese: 被告人の恋人である彼女は‥‥ すべてを目撃していた！
English: The defendant's very own lover is a witness to the whole thing!

Japanese: そう。彼女は事件が発生した瞬間 ‥‥殺人現場にいたのです！
English: That's right. She was at the scene of the crime when the murder took place!

Japanese: なんですっとおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: オキノドクでしたなあ‥‥ おじょうさん。
English: I'm sorry to break the bad news to you, my dear.

Japanese: “オキノドク”？ ‥‥とんでもない。
English: "Bad news"? You couldn't be more wrong.

Japanese: 私は‥‥ この瞬間を、待っていたのです。
English: Actually... I've been waiting for this.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですと‥‥？
English: You can't be serious...

Japanese: 千尋クン！ ど、どういうイミぢゃ‥‥？
English: Mia! What do you mean by that...?

Japanese: ここで、２０分間の 休憩をとりたいと思います。
English: I think this is a good point for us to stop at. Court will now enter a 20 minute recess.

Japanese: その後、美柳　ちなみさんの 証言を聞くことにしましょう！
English: Afterwards, we will listen to the testimony of Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: 同日　午前１１時５２分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: April 11, 11:52 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥スミマセンでした。 ‥‥ボク‥‥
English: Miss Fey... I-I'm sorry about what happened back there... I... I...

Japanese: ‥‥やっと、ホントのことを 話してくれたわね。なるほどくん。
English: It's alright. At least you told us the truth in the end, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ええ‥‥。だから、もう 安心ですよね？　ボク。
English: Yeah... So I guess I can start to relax then, huh?

Japanese: 安心なワケあるかいッ！ ダイジなコト、かくしおって‥‥
English: Relax, my boy? You can't be serious! After hiding such important facts...

Japanese: ううう‥‥。でも！ 次の証人、ちいちゃんでしょう？
English: B-But...! But the next witness is my Dollie, right?

Japanese: きっと、ボクたちのコト、 助けてくれちゃいますよ！
English: She'll save me! I just know she will!

Japanese: ‥‥どうして、そう思うの？
English: Why do you think that?

Japanese: え！　だって‥‥彼女、 ボクの運命のヒトだから！
English: Huh!? What do you mean!? She... She's the love of my life, that's why!

Japanese: 運命、ね‥‥。よかったら、 聞かせてくれないかしら？
English: The love of your life, huh...

Japanese: あなたと‥‥その、 美柳　ちなみさんのこと。
English: Would you mind telling me more about you and... Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne?

Japanese: ええ！　いいですとも！
English: Sure! No problem!

Japanese: ちいちゃんとは‥‥半年前、 この裁判所で出会いました。
English: Dollie and I... We first met about eight months ago right here in this very courthouse.

Japanese: ボク‥‥今、弁護士を 目指して勉強中なんですけど‥‥、
English: Actually, I'm studying to be a lawyer, on the side... Anyway!

Japanese: ある日、地下の資料室で、 バッタリ出会っちゃって。
English: One day, she and I just bumped into each other in the reading room downstairs.

Japanese: ホント、あれこそ“ウンメイ”って ヤツだったんですよねえ‥‥。
English: That's why I really think it was fate that brought us together.

Japanese: もお、ヒト目見たとたん、 いきなりイイ感じになっちゃって。
English: As soon as I first set eyes on her, I knew she was the one for me.

Japanese: ホラ、ホラ、見てくださいよ！
English: Oh, here! Take a look at this!

Japanese: そのときもらった、 コイビトのアカシなんです！
English: She gave this to me the day we met as a symbol of our love!

Japanese: あの日、彼女が首から さげていたんですけど。
English: She had been wearing it around her neck that day, but then...

Japanese: “あなたに、持っていてほしいの” なーんて言っちゃって。
English: ...she took it off, but before she gave it to me she said, "I want you to carry this."

Japanese: 彼女がくれた‥‥ “プレゼント”というワケかの。
English: So she gave it to you as a present, I see.

Japanese: ちっちゃなオモイデを入れるための ちっちゃなボトルなんですよ！
English: This darling little bottle is filled with memories of my darling little Dollie!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ちゃちな小ビンね。
English: It certainly is a little bottle alright.

Japanese: もお、うれしくて。会うヒト みんなに見せびらかしてるんです！
English: It makes me so happy, I show it to everyone I meet!

Japanese: ボクのシアワセを、みんなにも わけてあげたくて‥‥
English: I want to share my happiness with the whole world!

Japanese: 《ちなみのプレゼント》を なるほどくんから借りた。
English: Dahlia's Present borrowed from Phoenix Wright.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥
English: Um, anyway...

Japanese: それから、彼女と つきあうようになったのね？
English: So after that, you and Ms. Hawthorne started dating?

Japanese: 彼女、テレ屋さんだから‥‥ 会うたびに、ボクに言うんですよ。
English: Yeah, but she's so shy. Every time I see her, she always says the same thing to me.

Japanese: “やっぱりそれ、返して”って。
English: "Please give it back now."

Japanese: ヘンな女ぢゃの。一度あげたモノを 返せ、とは‥‥
English: What a strange girl, asking for a present back like that...

Japanese: ‥‥ところで、なるほどくん。
English: By the way, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あなたが、美柳　ちなみさんと 出会ったのは‥‥
English: The day you first met Dahlia Hawthorne, eight months ago...

Japanese: 半年前の、８月２７日のことね。
English: It wouldn't happen to have been on August 27th, would it?

Japanese: え‥‥。 ‥‥ど、どうしてそれを‥‥？
English: Huh...? Y-Yeah, it was... But how did you...?

Japanese: ８月２７日‥‥。この裁判所では、 こんな事件があったのよ。
English: This happened on August 27th, right here in this courthouse.

Japanese: それ‥‥新聞記事ですか？
English: What's this? A newspaper clipping?

Japanese: ええと、《裁判所で、殺人事件？》 さ、“さつじん”？
English: Let's see... "Murder in the Courthouse?" M-Murder?

Japanese: な、なんぢゃと！ ちょっと貸したまえッ！
English: What are you reading there!? Let me see that!
Oh, I see.

Japanese: 千尋クン。‥‥どうやら、 わかった気がするぞ。
English: Mia... I think I understand what you're trying to say.

Japanese: なぜチミが、急に今回の事件を 引き受ける、なぞと言い出したか。
English: And I think I understand why you suddenly took such a keen interest in this case.

Japanese: この記事の事件と、今回の事件‥‥ カンケイあり、というワケぢゃな。
English: You believe there is some relationship between these two cases. Am I correct?

Japanese: 《新聞記事》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Newspaper Clipping added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥すみません、先生。
English: I hope you don't mind, Mr. Grossberg...

Japanese: 私‥‥、ケリを つけなくちゃいけないんです。
English: I... I need to finish this myself.

Japanese: しかし‥‥これだけでは、 どうにも情報が足りん。
English: Ah, yes, but... I'm afraid what you have will not be enough, m'dear.

Japanese: よし。ワシが今から、 地下の資料室をあたってみよう。
English: I'll go and have a look at the downstairs reading room and see what else I can find.

Japanese: あ‥‥ありがとうございます！
English: Th-Thank you!

Japanese: 他ならぬチミのためぢゃ。 チカラのかぎり、協力するぞい！
English: I want to do whatever I can to be of help to you, Mia!

Japanese: とにかく、行くのぢゃ。 そろそろ休憩時間が終わる！
English: Well, it looks like recess is about over. We'd better all get moving!
(I guess so...)

Japanese: （‥‥ずいぶん長い《休憩時間》 だったような気がするわ‥‥）
English: (That recess sure seemed longer than 20 minutes though...)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、成歩堂　龍一に たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately...

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: ...to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!

Japanese: 綾里　真宵、綾里　春美、 華宮　霧緒、狩魔　冥、
English: Maya Fey, Pearl Fey, Adrian Andrews, Franziska von Karma.

Japanese: 御剣　怜侍、亜内　武文
English: Miles Edgeworth, Winston Payne
